mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production index indicates expanding manufacturing and mining output, which is a leading indicator of corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If over any rolling 3-year period, the Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 20-day returns is below 0.02, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds a high threshold of 8.5%, it signals severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return after triggering is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p>0.10), the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is increasing while the federal funds rate remains at near-zero levels, it signals an imminent monetary policy tightening cycle that will raise discount rates and reduce equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by >200,000 in two consecutive months and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
regime_hint: early expansion with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: If in triggered instances, median forward 20-day return is non-negative over a 5-year backtest, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of very high unemployment (UNRATE > 8%), a low federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary easing, which should eventually stimulate the economy and boost equity prices; thus, the combination of high UNRATE and low FEDFUNDS predicts positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return in such periods is not significantly different from zero at the 10% level, hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the rolling 3-year correlation of month-over-month RSAFS with forward 20-day returns is less than 0.1, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 600,000 units signals a weakening housing market, which is a leading indicator of economic contraction and will depress equity prices over the following week.
failure_condition: If average 5-day forward return after HOUST < 600 is non-negative over any 2-year period, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices are rising (indicating inflation) but the federal funds rate is unchanged at a low level, real interest rates become negative, encouraging investment in equities over cash; this interaction predicts positive forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% and FEDFUNDS unchanged month-over-month
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return in triggered events is negative or insignificant (p>0.05), hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where industrial production is expanding (INDPRO above 95), a high unemployment rate (above 8%) indicates that the labor market lags the supply side; this disparity will eventually resolve with falling unemployment, supporting future consumption and equity prices; thus, the combination of rising INDPRO and high UNRATE predicts positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If average 60-day forward return in triggered periods is non-positive over a full market cycle, hypothesis rejected.
